zh.Beddington, Surrey, England: 英國英格蘭素瑞貝丁頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Nicholas Carew於大約1332年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，他的父親Thomas Carew43歲，母親Mary Malmaines38歲。他於1352年之後與Lucy Willoughby在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1390年8月17日在英國素瑞貝丁頓享年59歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素瑞貝丁頓。

zh-hans.Beddington, Surrey, England: 英国英格兰素瑞貝丁頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Nicholas Carew于大约1332年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾时，他的父亲Thomas Carew43岁，母亲Mary Malmaines38岁。他于1352年之后与Lucy Willoughby在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1390年8月17日在英国素瑞貝丁頓享年59岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素瑞貝丁頓。

nameMeanings.name: Carew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aileen, James Patrick, Kieran.

Welsh (Pembrokeshire): habitational name from any of various minor places in Wales, in particular one near Pembroke with a major castle, named from Welsh caer ‘fort’ + rhiw ‘hill, slope’. The surname was taken to Ireland soon after the Norman invasion of England and was Gaelicized as de Carrún. In the 16th century a family called Carew from Devon played an important part in establishing the Tudor administration in Ireland.
Cornish (Somerset and Devon): from the cognate Cornish word kerrow (plural of ker ‘fort’), which occurs seven times as a placename in Cornwall.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Nicholas Carew was born about 1332, in Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Thomas Carew, was 43 and his mother, Mary Malmaines, was 38. He married Lucy Willoughby after 1352, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 17 August 1390, in Beddington, Surrey, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in Beddington, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Nicholas Carew est né environ 1332, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Carew, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Mary Malmaines, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Lucy Willoughby après 1352, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 17 août 1390, à Beddington, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Beddington, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Nicholas Carew um 1332 in Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Carew 43 und seine Mutter Mary Malmaines 38. Er hat nach 1352 in England Lucy Willoughby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 17. August 1390 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Beddington, Surrey, England und wurde in Beddington, Surrey, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Nicholas Carew è nato il circa 1332 nel Buckinghamshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Carew, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Mary Malmaines, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Lucy Willoughby dopo il 1352, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 17 agosto 1390, nel Beddington, Surrey, England, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Beddington, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Buckinghamshire, England: イギリスブクインガムシュアイア
ja.Beddington, Surrey, England: イギリスイギリススリーベッドイントン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Carewが約1332年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Thomas Carewは43歳，母Mary Malmainesは38歳であった。1352年以後にイギリスでLucy Willoughbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1390年8月17日にイギリススリーベッドイントンで59歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススリーベッドイントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Carew(이)가 대략 1332년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Carew(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Malmaines(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1352년 이후 영국에서 Lucy Willoughby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1390년 8월 17일 영국 수리 베딩턴에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수리 베딩턴에 묻혔다.

mn.Beddington, Surrey, England: Beddington, Sūþrīge, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Nicholas Carew nasceu aproximadamente 1332, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Carew, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Mary Malmaines, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucy Willoughby após 1352, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 17 de agosto de 1390, em Beddington, Surrey, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Beddington, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Nicholas Carew родился около 1332 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Carew было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Malmaines, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy Willoughby после 1352 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 17 августа 1390 г. в местности беддингтон, сурри, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности беддингтон, сурри, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Nicholas Carew nació aproximadamente 1332, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Carew, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Mary Malmaines, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Lucy Willoughby después de 1352, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 17 de agosto de 1390, en Beddington, Surrey, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Beddington, Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
